title: Daily Summary — 2026-05-01
body:
## Recent Activity

Synced window: 2026-04-30T11:51:43Z to 2026-05-01T11:51:43Z. Signals show 14 issues, 6 PRs, 1 discussion, and 0 commits updated.

Activity centered on orchestrator/sub-orchestrator design, comment-collapsing cleanup, agent task timeout/review-lane reliability, and the `acpx` update for Claude auto permission mode.

## Recently Active PRs

- Open: [#66 Support child issue sub-orchestrators](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/66) remained active with design discussion about avoiding `orchestrate` overload; follow-up work was split to [#76](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/76).
- Open: [#77 Add delegate_issue child orchestration](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/77) was created from [#76](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/76) to use an internal `delegate_issue` planner decision for child orchestration.
- Merged: [#60 Collapse old orchestrator handoff comments](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/60) and [#71 Update acpx for Claude auto permission mode](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/71).
- Closed without merge in the synced signals: [#45 Handle closed PR follow-up orchestration](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/45) and [#61 Support child orchestrator lane handoffs](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/61).

## Recently Active Issues

- [#51 Orchestrator to dispatch to another orchestrator](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/51) remained the main parent item for child/meta orchestration; implementation activity produced [#66](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/66).
- [#76 Better subagent orchestrate](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/76) was opened from PR #66 discussion and implemented via [#77](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/77).
- [#70 Codex review lane can hang in acpx and block synthesis](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/70) and [#72 Adding timeout for agent tasks](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/72) captured reliability/timeout follow-up.
- [#65 Refactor generated comment minimization helpers](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/65) was clarified as an umbrella for [#67](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/67) and [#68](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/68).
- Closed in the synced signals: [#59](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/59) and [#69](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/69).

## Recently Active Discussions

- [#63 Daily Summary — 2026-04-30](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/discussions/63) was updated with project-management summaries and managed-label activity.

## Follow-ups

1. Review [#77](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/77) as the cleaner replacement path for the child-orchestration design concerns raised on [#66](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/66).
2. Decide the timeout strategy for [#70](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/70) and [#72](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/72), since hung review lanes can block synthesis.
3. Use [#65](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/65) to consolidate comment-minimization behavior for inline review comments and repeated `/fix-pr` comments.

---
*Generated by the agent-daily-summary workflow*

nodes.body:
## Project Management Summary

- Mode: `labels applied`
- Open items assessed: `25 issues, 4 pull requests`
- Managed labels: `priority/*`, `effort/*`

### Top Triage Queue

- `pull_request#77` Add delegate_issue child orchestration — `priority/p1`, `effort/high` — Active replacement PR for the child-orchestration design, addressing the concern that `orchestrate` should stay a public route while child delegation becomes an internal decision. Label changes: none.
- `issue#76` Better subagent orchestrate — `priority/p1`, `effort/high` — High-impact architectural correction with an implementation PR already opened; still worth keeping near the top until the replacement PR is reviewed and landed. Label changes: none.
- `issue#75` Agent review-body commands can be cancelled by sibling inline review-comment events — `priority/p1`, `effort/medium` — User-facing command loss in a normal GitHub review flow; likely localized to workflow concurrency keying but important for reliability. Label changes: none.
- `issue#72` Adding timeout for agent tasks — `priority/p1`, `effort/high` — Shared runtime timeout/reporting work affects stuck orchestrator and handoff visibility; broad but important reliability work. Label changes: none.
- `issue#70` Codex review lane can hang in acpx and block synthesis — `priority/p1`, `effort/medium` — Concrete reliability bug blocking review synthesis; likely partially addressed by the timeout work but still independently actionable. Label changes: none.
- `issue#43` Allow /orchestrate follow-up patches from merged or closed PRs — `priority/p1`, `effort/high` — Important orchestrator continuity capability across PR lifecycle states, with broad workflow implications. Label changes: none.
- `issue#26` Automatically report issues on GitHub — `priority/p1`, `effort/high` — High-value automation and observability work with broad workflow impact. Label changes: none.
- `pull_request#62` Add first-run onboarding setup workflow — `priority/p1`, `effort/high` — Addresses install/onboarding gaps for new repositories; open draft remains high leverage for adoption. Label changes: none.
- `issue#23` Agent should create tags upon install/first run — `priority/p1`, `effort/high` — Parent onboarding issue with implementation PR open; should stay prominent until PR #62 is resolved. Label changes: none.
- `pull_request#66` Support child issue sub-orchestrators — `priority/p2`, `effort/medium` — Earlier draft appears superseded by the cleaner `delegate_issue` design in PR #77; remaining work is likely reconciliation or closure rather than continuing the old design. Label changes: remove `priority/p1`, `effort/high`; add `priority/p2`, `effort/medium`.

### Label Changes

One managed-label update is needed: downgrade superseded draft PR #66 from active high-priority/high-effort implementation to normal-priority reconciliation work. All other assessed items already have one appropriate managed priority label and one appropriate managed effort label.

```json
{
  "label_changes": [
    {
      "kind": "pull_request",
      "number": 66,
      "add": ["priority/p2", "effort/medium"],
      "remove": ["priority/p1", "effort/high"]
    }
  ]
}
```

### Notes

Assumption: PR #66 is superseded by PR #77 based on issue #76’s request to implement a new version with the `delegate_issue` design. No issues or PRs were skipped. No labels, comments, issues, PRs, commits, or discussions were mutated by the agent.

### Managed Label Application

- Applied 4 managed priority/effort label operation(s).
